title: Grilled Lemon-Herb Chicken
steps: Marinate chicken with lemon juice, olive oil, herbs, and seasoning.
Grill chicken until marked and cooked through.
Rest briefly, slice, and hold warm for service.

title: Beef Bourguignon
steps: Brown beef in batches and remove from the pan.
Cook mushrooms and onions, then deglaze with wine.
Braise beef with vegetables until tender and sauce is reduced.

title: Cream of Mushroom Soup
steps: Saute mushrooms and onions until moisture evaporates.
Add stock and simmer until flavors develop.
Blend partially, finish with cream, and season.

title: Beef Meatloaf with Jus
steps: Mix beef, breadcrumbs, eggs, and seasoning until just combined.
Shape into loaves and bake until cooked through.
Slice and serve with hot beef jus.

title: Coffee-Rubbed Beef Tenderloin, shallot butter (GF)
steps: Season tenderloin with coffee rub and kosher salt.
Sear and roast to desired doneness.
Rest, slice, and finish with shallot butter.

title: Miso and Soy Chilean Sea Bass

ingredients.name: (4 ounce) fillets fresh sea bass, about 1 inch thick

steps: Whisk together sake, mirin, miso paste, brown sugar, and soy sauce in a bowl.
Place sea bass fillets in a large resealable plastic bag; pour marinade over fillets. Chill in the refrigerator for 3 to 6 hours.
Preheat the oven's broiler and set the oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source. Prop the oven door to remain slightly ajar.
Arrange fillets on a baking sheet; discard marinade.
Broil sea bass in the preheated oven until fish flakes easily with a fork, 7 to 9 minutes.
Sprinkle with chopped green onions to serve.

title: Spinach and Ricotta Manicotti

ingredients.name: (10 ounce) package frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed dry

steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
Mix ricotta, spinach, onion, egg, parsley, pepper, and garlic powder together in a large bowl. Stir in 1 cup mozzarella and 1/4 cup Parmesan. Stuff mixture into uncooked manicotti shells.

Stir spaghetti sauce and water together in a bowl. Spread 1 cup sauce over the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish. Arrange stuffed manicotti in a single layer in the dish. Cover with remaining sauce, then sprinkle with remaining mozzarella and Parmesan.
Bake in the preheated oven until shells are tender yet firm to the bite, 45 to 55 minutes.

title: Ginger Jasmine Rice
steps: In a colander, rinse the 1 cup jasmine rice (not instant)* well, shaking off excess water.

Combine 2 1/4 cup water or low-sodium chicken broth, rinsed rice, fresh grated 1 tablespoon ginger, grated 1 clove garlic, 1 tablespoon butter, 1/2 teaspoon kosher salt and 1/2 teaspoon ground black pepper in a medium sauce pan.
Bring to a low boil and then lower heat to a low simmer.
Cover and allow to cook for 20-25 minutes.
Fluff with a fork and serve.
Top with chopped 2 tablespoons scallions or serve with your favorite meal.
If you've tried this recipe, please come back to leave us a comment and give it a star rating!

title: Corn Soup

ingredients.name: kosher salt and white pepper blend

steps: Inspect and wash all vegetables; dice aromatics evenly for consistent cooking.
Sweat aromatics in oil or butter over medium heat until translucent with no browning.
Add primary vegetable and stock; simmer until completely tender and flavors are developed.
Blend in batches until smooth, then strain only if a refined texture is required.
Finish with cream or alternate dairy, adjust seasoning, and cool rapidly if not serving immediately.
Reheat gently for service and garnish each portion consistently.

title: Citrus and Shrimp Lemon Lavender Cups

ingredients.name: fresh herbs, citrus, or service garnish

steps: Season shrimp with dried lavender and kosher salt, then saute in olive oil until opaque and cooked through.
Combine mint, lemon zest, lemon juice, orange segments, orange zest, cucumber, and seasoning for the citrus garnish.
Toss the hot shrimp with the citrus-cucumber mixture and spoon into separated endive leaves for service.
Assemble with garnish so each portion is consistent for service.
Taste one completed portion for seasoning and texture before pickup.
Hold cold according to the dish and refresh garnish at service.

Taste for doneness, salt, acid, and texture before transferring to hotel pans.
Hold hot without overcooking; refresh with herbs or lemon before service.
Portion evenly alongside the assigned entree.

title: Roasted Root Vegetables (Carrot/Yam/Parsnip/Turnip)
steps: Wash, trim, and cut vegetables into uniform pieces for even cooking.
Blanch or par-cook vegetables if needed; shock and drain well for service hold.
Finish in saute pan, oven, or steamer with fat, aromatics, and seasoning.
Taste for doneness, salt, acid, and texture before transferring to hotel pans.
Hold hot without overcooking; refresh with herbs or lemon before service.
Portion evenly alongside the assigned entree.

title: Butter Mashed Potatoes
steps: Rinse, trim, or cut starch as required and measure liquid accurately.
Cook starch with stock, fat, aromatics, and salt until tender and fully hydrated.
Fluff, fold, or mash gently to avoid breaking down the finished texture.
Adjust seasoning and moisture before holding for service.
Hold covered at safe temperature and refresh with herbs or butter before pickup.
Portion consistently for 24 plates.

title: Apple Pie
steps: Scale ingredients accurately and prepare pans, molds, or service vessels.
Mix base ingredients in the correct order until smooth and evenly incorporated.
Bake, chill, simmer, or set dessert according to the preparation style.
Cool safely and portion into 24 consistent servings.
Prepare garnish or sauce separately and hold until service.
Finish each portion neatly with garnish just before pickup.

title: MANHATTAN CLAM CHOWDER (TOMATO BASE)

ingredients.name: Russet potatoes, peeled and diced into 1/2-inch cubes
oyster crackers and cooked bacon for serving, optional

steps: Heat a large pot over medium heat. Add the bacon and cook, stirring occasionally, until crisp.
Remove the bacon from the pot and drain on paper towels.
Add the onions, carrots and celery to the pan. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 6-8 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Place the tomato paste, thyme, bay leaf, clam juice, broth and potatoes in the pot. Stir to combine. Bring to a simmer.
Cook for 10-15 minutes or until potatoes are tender.
Stir in the tomatoes and baby clams. Season the soup with salt and pepper to taste.
Simmer for 5 more minutes. Stir in the reserved bacon. Top with chopped parsley. Add oyster crackers and more bacon if desired, then serve.

title: BOSTON CREAM PIE
steps: Prepare the cake batter with flour, baking powder, salt, butter, sugar, eggs, vanilla, and milk, then bake until set and cooled.
Cook the pastry cream by heating milk, tempering with sugar, egg yolks, and cornstarch, then cooking until thickened.
Finish pastry cream with butter and vanilla, then chill until firm enough to spread.
Split the cooled cake into layers and fill with pastry cream.
Pour warm chocolate ganache over the top and chill until set before slicing.

title: WHITE BEAN DIP WITH TOASTED PITA CHIPS

ingredients.name: scallions, thinly sliced, white and light green parts separated from dark greens
to 2 tablespoons chile crisp or chile paste, to taste
(15-ounce) cans white beans, such as cannellini or Great Northern, drained and rinsed
fine sea salt, plus more to taste
ounces sharp white Cheddar, grated (about 2 cups)

steps: Heat oven to 475 degrees. In a 10-inch ovenproof skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high. Add garlic and white and light green scallion slices, reserving the dark greens for later. Cook until lightly golden, 1 to 3 minutes. Carefully stir in tomato paste and chile crisp, and cook for 1 minute.

Stir in the beans and salt. Taste mixture, adding more salt and chili crisp as needed (remember that the cheese will add saltiness, so don’t overdo the salt here). Sprinkle cheese evenly over the top. Bake for 6 to 10 minutes, or until the cheese has melted and browned in spots. If you’d like to toast the top further, run the skillet under the broiler for 1 to 2 minutes. Sprinkle with reserved scallion greens and serve.

title: TOMATO BASIL PIZETTE

ingredients.name: plain flour, plus extra for sprinkling
mature cheddar cheese, grated and divided
chopped fresh chives, plus more for serving
chopped fresh basil, plus more for serving

steps: To make the shortcrust pasty, place the plain flour, the diced butter, 1 cup of cheddar cheese, the black pepper, and the dried
thyme into a food processor. Pulse the mixture in short bursts until it begins to resemble breadcrumbs. Keep pulsing in short
bursts until the ingredients begin to form larger clumps. It may be necessary to add a tablespoon of water if this does not
happen. As soon as the pastry begins to form, stop pulsing, remove the pastry from the food processor and form it into a ball.
Wrap the ball of pastry in plastic wrap and place it in the fridge for 30 minutes to harden.
Remove the pastry from the fridge, coat a clean surface with little flour, unwrap the pastry and place it onto the floured
surface. Using a rolling pin, roll it out until it is roughly ⅓-inch thick.
Carefully lift the pastry and place it into a 9-inch pie dish. Press it into the shape of the dish and then trim the edges. Place the
pastry back into the fridge for 15 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 400 F. Line the pastry with baking paper and add pie weights, then bake the pastry for 20 minutes.
Remove the pie weights and the parchment paper, and continue to bake the pastry case for another 10 minutes until it is set
and lightly colored. Place to one side and allow to cool.
Reduce the oven temperature to 300 F.
To prepare the tomatoes, slice them into ½-inch thick pieces and place them onto a large baking tray. Drizzle with 1
tablespoon of olive oil, sprinkle with the salt, and add the sliced garlic and the fresh thyme stalks to the baking tray. Bake in
the oven for 35 to 40 minutes. This will help to remove some of the excess liquid in the tomatoes.
When the tomatoes are ready, remove them from the oven and allow them to cool to room temperature.
On the stove, using a medium-sized frying pan, gently fry the diced onion and bacon with 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Cook until
the onion is translucent and the bacon is turning crisp and golden around the edges. Leave the mixture to cool.
Meanwhile, in a medium-sized bowl, mix together the mayonnaise and the egg until they are combined. Then, stir in the
cooled onion and bacon, 2 cups of cheddar, the fresh chives, and the fresh basil.
To prepare the pie for baking, preheat the oven to 375 F. Gather together the cooled pie crust, the tomatoes, the mayonnaise
mixture, and the breadcrumbs. Begin by spreading half of the mayonnaise mixture onto the base of the pie crust. Sprinkle 1
tablespoon of breadcrumbs on top of the mayonnaise, and then layer half of the tomatoes on top. Repeat the process with the
other half of the ingredients, first the mayonnaise, then the breadcrumbs, and finally the remaining tomatoes.
Place the tomato pie into the oven and bake for 40 to 45 minutes, checking occasionally to make sure that the pie crust isn't
browning too much. If it is, cover the pie with tin foil for the remainder of the cook time.
Once the pie is ready, remove it from the oven and let it sit for 30 minutes, then optionally top with more fresh chives and/or
basil before serving

title: THAI CHICKEN NOODLE SOUP

ingredients.name: lime, cut into wedges, to serve

steps: In a large pot on medium heat, melt the coconut oil, then add the onion and carrots and cook for 3-4 minutes until tender.
Add the red bell pepper, mushrooms, garlic, ginger, and chili (optional), and cook for 2-3 minutes until slightly tender. Then add the curry paste and stir to coat all the vegetables.

Pour in the broth, stir to combine with the vegetables, and bring to a gentle boil. Add the raw chicken breasts to the pot, submerge them in the broth, reduce the heat to a simmer, and cook for 20 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.

After 20 minutes, turn down the heat, remove the chicken breasts from the pot, and place them on a cutting board. Using two forks, shred the chicken into fine strips or bite-size pieces and set aside.

Bring the pot of broth back up to a boil, then add the rice noodles and cook for half of the recommended cooking time (e.g. if your noodles need to be cooked for 8 minutes, cook them for 4 minutes). As the noodles cook, stir the soup regularly to ensure the noodles do not stick together.

Once the noodles have cooked halfway, pour in the coconut milk and fish sauce (optional), and return the shredded chicken to the pot. Stir to combine, and continue to cook for the remaining time or until the noodles are tender.
Once the noodles are cooked, turn off the heat, add the chopped fresh cilantro, and stir to combine. Taste and adjust the seasoning with additional salt as needed.
Once cooked, portion the chicken noodle soup into bowls and serve with additional cilantro and fresh lime juice as desired.

title: SEARED CATFISH
steps: Pelez et hachez grossièrement les échalotes. Disposez-les dans une casserole.
Pressez le citron et ajoutez le jus dans la casserole. Versez également le vin blanc. Faites chauffer à feu moyen afin que le liquide s'évapore.
Retirez la casserole du feu. Ajoutez le beurre et mélangez afin qu'il fonde. Délayez avec la crème liquide.
Placez la casserole sur le feu au bain-marie. Réchauffez la sauce quelques minutes. Nappez votre poisson avec ce beurre blanc maison.

title: CHICKEN CAESAR SALAD

ingredients.name: romaine or salad greens, washed, dried, and chopped
large chicken breast, seasoned and cooked

steps: Add greens to a large mixing bowl. Layer crumbled bacon, hard-boiled eggs, chicken breast, and cherry tomatoes. Sprinkle on ½ tablespoon grated parmesan and 1 tablespoon shaved parmesan with 1 cup of croutons.
Drizzle the desired amount of dressing and toss (or serve undressed and dress individual servings).
Garnish with the remaining ½ tablespoon grated parmesan cheese and 1 tablespoon shaved parmesan. Optional: sprinkle with salt and black pepper.
Serving size: 2 large dinner salads or 4 side salads.

title: Thai Fish Cakes with Cilantro Dip
steps: Flake the fish and put into a medium sized bowl.
Add the egg, mayo, red curry paste, cilantro, green onion, lime juice and zest of lime, and ginger.
Mix well, then stir in the breadcrumbs.
Divide the mixture into 4 equal pieces, then roll each piece into a ball then flatten into a patty shape.
Heat a large skillet to medium heat, then add a bit of oil to the pan.
When the pan is hot, add the fish cakes and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side.
To make the tartar sauce, mix together the mayo, minced capers, lime zest and lime juice, and minced green onion then season with sea salt and pepper.

title: 3 - Sisters Indigenous Soup

ingredients.name: medium yellow onion, peeled and diced
red potatoes, diced (no need to peel)
salt and fresh cracked black pepper, to taste
jalapeño pepper, minced (leave out for less heat)
grated Parmesan or Asiago cheese, optional

steps: Heat the oil in a large soup pot or Dutch oven and add the diced onion. Saute for five minutes, stirring often, until the onion starts to soften. Add the garlic and cook a minute or two longer.
Add the potatoes, chipotle powder, cumin, and bay leaves to the pot and cook a couple of more minutes, stirring almost constantly.
Add the broth and tomatoes to the pot and bring to a simmer. Add the jalapeños, if using, at this point too. Bring to a boil.
Lower the heat and simmer just until the potatoes are just tender, about 8-12 minutes. You can cover the pot if you like, but make sure you're cooking at a simmer, not a full boil.

Add the zucchini, summer squash, corn and beans, and bring the soup back to a bubble. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Add more chipotle or cumin at this point if you like. Cover and let the soup simmer for just a few minutes. You just want to take the raw edge off your veggies. When they taste just barely tender, the soup is finished. Add water if your soup seems too thick.
Serve the soup topped with fresh parsley, and a sprinkle of cheese, if you like.

title: COCONUT SHRIMP WITH LIME DIP

ingredients.name: fresh or frozen shrimp (peeled and deveined)
water (if needed to thin out the dip)

steps: Prehat a non-stick skillet with 3-4 tbsp vegetable oil in it over medium-high heat.
Prep your shrimp by patting them dry with a paper towel.
Season them with salt.
Set up three shallow bowls or pie pans.
In the first, mix the cornstarch and flour together.
In the second, put the two lightly beaten eggs.
In the third, mix together the panko and coconut.
Working one shrimp at a time, dip them first in the flour mixture, then the eggs, then the panko and coconut, making sure each is coated thouroughly.
Then place in the pan.
Keep dredging the shrimp until there's no room left in the pan.
Cook for two minutes per side, and place on a wire cooling rack over a baking sheet.
Continue with the remaining shrimp.
You may have to add extra oil to the pan in between batches.
Serve with honey lime dipping sauce.
Add all the ingredients to a small bowl and whisk to combine. If it's too thick add a ½ tsp of water.

title: CARROT AND ORANGE SOUP

ingredients.name: Zest and juice of 1 large orange
Salt and white pepper to taste

steps: Chop the onion and slice the carrots.
Zest and juice the orange.
If using fresh thyme, strip the leaves from the stems.
Toast the slivered almonds in a dry pan over medium heat until lightly golden and fragrant. Set aside.
In a medium pot, melt the butter or heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced carrots, vegetable broth, and thyme. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, until the carrots are very tender.
Remove the pot from the heat. Carefully blend the soup using an immersion blender until it's velvety smooth.
Stir in the orange zest and orange juice.
Season with salt and white pepper to taste. Gently reheat if necessary.

title: MAPLE GLAZED SALMON FILET WITH

ingredients.name: (500 g) salmon filet, bones removed and cut into 4 pieces
. (20 mL) Dijon mustard or grainy Dijon mustard

steps: Preheat oven to 425 F (220°C). Arrange salmon in 8-inch (20-cm) baking dish.
Microwave Becel® Buttery Taste margarine at HIGH until melted about 15 seconds. Stir in maple syrup, Dijon mustard and soy sauce. Pour over salmon.
Bake until salmon flakes with a fork, about 12 minutes. Serve, if desired, with hot rice and vegetables.

title: CHICKEN CORDON BLEU
steps: Combine the flour and paprika in a shallow pan.
Pound chicken breasts to 1/4 inch thick, then place one slice of Swiss and one slice of ham on each breast, leaving the edges clear.
Roll the chicken around the filling, coat each roll in the flour mixture, and secure with toothpicks.
Melt butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat and brown the chicken rolls on all sides, 5 to 8 minutes.
Add wine and bouillon, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until the chicken reaches 165 F, about 30 minutes.
Transfer chicken to a warm platter and remove toothpicks.
Whisk cream with cornstarch, add it slowly to the skillet juices, and cook 3 to 5 minutes until thickened.
Spoon the sauce over the chicken.

title: ROAST CHICKEN WITH PAPRIKA
steps: Preheat the oven to 325 F.
Pat the chicken dry thoroughly.
Mix smoked paprika, melted butter, garlic powder, salt, and pepper; spread the mixture over the entire chicken.
Place the chicken in a shallow baking pan.
Warm honey and lemon juice in a small pot until the honey dissolves, then set aside.
Roast for 1 hour to 1 hour 15 minutes, basting with the honey-lemon glaze after 35 minutes and every 15 minutes after that.
Cook until juices run clear and the breast and thigh register about 165 F.

title: BLACK FOREST CAKE
steps: Preheat oven to 350 F; grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans, line bottoms with parchment, and chill a mixing bowl for cream.
Whisk flour, sugar, cocoa, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl.
Add eggs, milk, oil, and vanilla and beat until combined.
Divide batter between prepared pans and bake about 35 minutes, until a tester comes out clean.
Cool cake layers 10 minutes in pans, loosen edges, invert, and cool fully for 1 to 2 hours.
Drain cherries, reserving 1/2 cup juice; combine reserved juice, cherries, sugar, and cornstarch in a saucepan.
Cook cherry mixture over low heat, stirring, until thickened; stir in vanilla and chill completely.
Whip cream with confectioners sugar in the chilled bowl to stiff peaks.
Split each cake layer horizontally; crumble one layer and reserve crumbs.
Reserve 1 1/2 cups frosting for piping, then layer cake rounds with frosting and cherry topping.
Frost the sides, press reserved cake crumbs onto the sides, pipe the reserved frosting around the top and bottom edges, and spoon remaining cherry topping over the top.

title: ROASTED RED PEPPER HUMMUS AND PITA

ingredients.name: bell peppers, (seeded and cut in 1-inch strips,)
pepper, (seeded, and sliced in half length wise (optional))
cooked chickpeas, (canned chickpeas are fine, drained and rinsed)
to 1 tsp smoked paprika, (adjust to your liking)

steps: Preheat oven to 450 F (skip this part if using jarred roasted red peppers and jalapeno.
Roast the peppers: Place the red bell pepper strips and jalapeno in a small baking dish or cast-iron skillet.
Drizzle generously with olive oil.
Bake in a heated oven for 15 to 20 minutes or until tender and well-charred (turn peppers over halfway through).
Remove from oven and let cool.
In the bowl of a fitted with an S-blade, add the roasted bell pepper strips from one bell pepper and jalapeno along with the chickpeas, garlic, tahini, sumac, smoked paprika, and lemon juice.
Drizzle a little extra virgin olive oil.
Run the processor until you reach the desired creamy paste consistency.
Test and adjust seasoning, if needed.
Run the processor again to combine.
Serve: Transfer to a serving dish, and spoon swoops and swirls into the hummus.
Mince the remaining pepper strips and sprinkle them over the hummus.
Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil, and sprinkle a pinch of paprika or sumac for garnish, if you like.
Serve with warm pita and sliced veggies.

title: SPICY TUNA AND CRISPY RICE CRACKER
steps: Cook sushi rice according to package directions. While warm, mix in the rice vinegar and sugar.
Press the seasoned rice firmly into a small rectangular pan or baking dish, about 1/2 inch thick. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour, or until firm.

While the rice chills, prepare the tuna. In a medium bowl, combine the finely chopped tuna, Japanese mayonnaise, sriracha, sesame oil, and sliced scallions. Mix gently until just combined. Refrigerate until ready to use.
Once the rice is firm, cut it into small rectangles (about 1x2 inches).
In a large skillet, heat about an inch of vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
Carefully fry the rice patties in batches for 2-3 minutes per side, until golden brown and crispy. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.

title: SEARED HALIBUT WITH CHERRY TOMATO&OLIVE

ingredients.name: neutral oil, such as avocado or vegetable

steps: Pat halibut fillets dry and season with salt and pepper.
Sear halibut in a hot oiled skillet until browned and just cooked through.
Serve with tortilla chips for dipping.

title: PASTA WITH SUMMER VEGETABLES WITH LEMON PARMESAN SAUCE

ingredients.name: short pasta (like penne, bowties, or rotini)
medium yellow squash, sliced into half-moons
freshly grated Parmesan cheese (plus more for serving)
fresh basil leaves, chopped or torn
Salt and black pepper to taste
A pinch of crushed red pepper flakes

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook your pasta according to the package directions until it is just al dente.
Before draining the pasta, carefully scoop out and reserve at least 1/2 cup of the starchy pasta water. Drain the pasta and set it aside.

Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the zucchini and yellow squash, sauteing for 3 to 4 minutes until they begin to soften and slightly brown. Stir in the minced garlic, peas, and cherry tomatoes, cooking for another 1 to 2 minutes until the garlic is fragrant and the tomatoes just begin to soften. Season lightly with salt and pepper, then transfer the veggies to a plate.

Turn the skillet heat down to low. Melt the butter in the pan, then stir in the lemon juice, lemon zest, and half of your reserved pasta water. Whisk in the grated Parmesan cheese until it melts smoothly into the liquid.

Add the cooked pasta and sauteed vegetables back into the skillet. Toss everything together vigorously over low heat so the cheese, lemon, and pasta starch merge into a glossy sauce that clings to the noodles. If the pasta looks too dry, splash in a little more of your reserved pasta water.

Turn off the heat. Stir in the fresh basil and optional red pepper flakes. Divide among bowls and finish with an extra sprinkle of Parmesan cheese and a crack of fresh black pepper. This recipe makes 4 servings

title: PEACH FRIED PIES
steps: In a large bowl, sift together flour and salt.
Cut in butter and shortening until mixture resembles large crumbs.
Add water and stir until a dough begins to form.
Turn dough out onto a work surface and begin kneading until you have a soft, uniform ball of dough. (dough will be very crumbly at first) Cut dough into 8 equal size pieces and roll into a ball.
Place dough balls on a baking sheet and press them into a disc.
Cover and refrigerate until chilled, about 30 minutes or until ready to use.
In a large saucepan, add peaches, granulated sugar, and brown sugar.
Toss to coat peaches in sugar.
Bring to a simmer over medium heat until peaches are tender enough to mash.
Mash the peaches (I like to mash some and then cut the rest into small chunks) Allow the peaches to continue simmering until thickened and the juices have dissolved.
Stir in butter and lemon juice.
Stir in cornstarch if needed.
Remove from heat and allow to cool completely.
In a large bowl, stir together powdered sugar and milk until silky. (may need to add more milk) Stir in a pinch of salt and vanilla extract. Set aside until ready to use.
To assemble the pies, roll a dough ball out to about 5-6 inches wide.
Place 2 tablespoons of peach filling in the center.
Fold the dough over the peach filling and press the edges of the dough together firmly.
Use a pizza cutter to trim the edges to make them neater.
Crimp the edges with a fork to further seal the pie.
Repeat these steps with the rest of the pies.
Heat oil in a deep-fryer or deep skillet to 350 F.
Once the oil is hot, carefully place 2-3 pies in the oil and fry until lightly golden, about 3-4 minutes. (flip midway if needed) Place fried pies on a cooling rack.
When the pies are done frying, brush one side with the glaze.
Then, once the glaze has hardened, brush them once again.
Serve once the final glaze has hardened.

title: SHAVED FENNEL AND ORANGE SALAD
steps: Use a sharp knife or mandoline to shave the fennel very thinly.
Segment the oranges over a bowl, reserving the juices.
Whisk reserved orange juice with olive oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Toss shaved fennel with orange segments and dressing.
Finish with fennel fronds before serving.

title: CUCUMBER AND DILL SALAD
steps: Place cucumber slices in a colander, sprinkle generously with salt, and rest about 10 minutes until moisture beads on the slices.
Drain the released cucumber liquid.
Whisk sour cream, vinegar, yogurt, mayonnaise, lime juice, sugar, and dill until smooth.
Fold cucumbers and sliced red onion into the dressing until evenly coated.

title: CHICKEN MARSALA IN MUSHROOM SAUCE

ingredients.name: , skinless chicken breasts halves (about 1.5 pounds total weight)
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
thick-sliced brown or white button mushrooms
chopped fresh thyme (or 1 teaspoon dried thyme)
dry Marsala wine (or 50/50 mixture of brandy and dry white wine)

steps: To slice chicken scaloppine: Arrange 3 boneless, skinless chicken breasts halves sideways and slice them in half. With the knife blade parallel to the cutting surface, slice them in half again, making 4 scaloppine slices per chicken breast.
Generously sprinkle both sides of the chicken with salt and pepper.
Spread ½ cup all-purpose flour on a large plate.
Dredge the chicken in the flour to coat both sides, shaking off any excess.
Transfer the floured chicken to a piece of parchment or wax paper as you go.

Place a large (preferably 12-inch) skillet over medium-high heat. Add one tablespoon each of the butter and olive oil. When the butter is melted and stops foaming, add 1 pound thick-sliced brown or white button mushrooms to the pan.
Cook in one layer without stirring until the mushrooms are golden brown on one side.
Stir in ½ cup chopped shallot, 1-2 cloves finely chopped garlic, 2 teaspoons chopped fresh thyme and ¾ teaspoon salt.
Cook 2-3 more minutes, stirring frequently, until the shallots are translucent.
Transfer the mushrooms to a bowl.
Return the skillet to medium-high heat and add the remaining tablespoon of butter and olive oil.
When the butter has melted, add the dredged chicken pieces to the pan.
Cook about 5 minutes per side, until golden brown on both sides (they'll still be a undercooked at this point).
Note: If you're pan isn't large enough to hold all the chicken without crowding, cook them in 2 batches.
Pour ½ cup dry Marsala wine into the pan — it should begin to boil instantly.
Cook until the wine is almost completely evaporated and reduced to a sticky glaze.
Add ½ cup heavy cream, ¼ cup chicken broth and the cooked mushrooms (including all the juice they exuded), nudging the chicken here and there while gently stirring the sauce.
Bring to a simmer and cook until the sauce is lightly thickened, and the chicken is cooked through, 5-7 minutes
Remove the pan from the heat. Sprinkle the parsley over the chicken and serve.

title: OPEN FACED ROAST BEEF DIP

ingredients.name: (6-inch) French or hoagie rolls, sliced in half diagonally
Minced fresh flat-leaf parsley, for serving (optional)

steps: Generously season the steak with salt and pepper.

Heat a large cast-iron skillet over high heat and sear the steak until it reaches medium-rare, depending on thickness about 3 minutes per side, or until it reaches 135 F on an instant-read thermometer.
Let rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the steak into strips, as thin as possible against the grain and set aside.

Make the au jus. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, whisk the beef stock, thyme, salt, sherry, Worcestershire, garlic powder, and onion powder, until fully combined. Simmer, stirring occasionally for 5 minutes.
Butter the inside of each roll, and sprinkle with the 2 teaspoons garlic powder. Place open-faced on a rimmed baking sheet.
Turn the oven broiler to HIGH. Quickly dip the strips of steak into the au jus and place in even amounts onto one side of each roll. Sprinkle ½ cup of the cheese on each sandwich.
Place the sheet pan under the broiler until the cheese begins to melt, watching carefully, about 2 minutes. Remove from the oven and sprinkle with parsley, if desired.
Ladle ¾ cup of au jus into small ramekins. Serve with the warm sandwiches for dipping.

title: LEMON BAKED COD
steps: Preheat oven to 400 degrees F.
Mix together the lemon juice, olive oil, and melted butter in a shallow bowl (do not add the garlic yet). Set aside.
In another shallow bowl, mix the all-purpose flour, spices, salt and pepper. Set next to the lemon sauce.
Pat the fish dry. Dip the fish in the lemon sauce then dip it in the flour mixture. Shake off excess flour. Reserve the lemon sauce for later.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a cast iron skillet (or an oven-safe pan) over medium-high heat (watch the oil to be sure it is shimmering but not smoking).
Add the fish and sear on each side to give it some color, but do not fully cook (about 2 minutes on each side).
Remove the skillet from heat.
To the remaining lemon sauce, add the minced garlic and mix. Drizzle all over the fish fillets.

title: ROASTED BABY TOP CARROTS
steps: Placeholder recipe created to link the current menu assignment. Replace with the approved production recipe before service.
notes: Placeholder only. Ingredients, quantities, and method still need chef review.

title: GARLIC MASHED POTATOES

ingredients.name: potatoes, preferably Yukon Gold or another yellow, waxy potato

steps: Preheat oven to 400 F.
Remove loose papery skin from the garlic head, slice off the tops of the cloves, drizzle with olive oil, salt lightly, wrap in foil, and roast 30 to 40 minutes until soft and beginning to brown.
Peel potatoes, cut into 1-inch chunks, place in a saucepan with 1/2 teaspoon salt, and cover with cold water.
Bring potatoes to a boil, reduce to a simmer, and cook about 15 minutes until fork-tender.
Warm cream and butter together until the butter melts.
Drain potatoes, return them to the pot over low heat, squeeze in the roasted garlic, and mash.
Add warm cream and butter and mash to the desired texture without overmixing.

title: BANANA CREAM PIE

ingredients.name: pie crust (what I used) or all butter pie crust*
optional egg wash: 1 large egg white beaten with 1 Tablespoon milk
(28g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature

steps: Make the pie crust through step 5 according to my directions and video tutorial in my pie crust recipe.
Make pie dough in advance because it needs to chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before rolling out (step 2).
The recipe makes 2 crusts, and you only need 1 for this pie, so save the other for another pie.
On a floured work surface, roll out one of the discs of chilled dough.
Turn the dough about a quarter turn after every few rolls until you have a circle 12 inches in diameter.
Carefully place the dough into a 9-inch pie dish.
Tuck it in with your fingers, making sure it is completely smooth.
To make a lovely thick edge, I do not trim excess dough around the edges.
Instead, fold the excess dough back over the edge and use your hands to mold the edge into a rim around the pie.

Crimp the edges with a fork or use your fingers to flute the edges. (Review this how to crimp and flute pie crust page if you need extra help with this step.) If dough is too warm, fluting/crimping will be difficult, so you can always chill it in the refrigerator for 5–10 minutes first.
After fluting/crimping the edges, chill the pie dough in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes and up to 5 days.
Cover the pie crust with plastic wrap if chilling for longer than 30 minutes.
Chilling the shaped dough helps prevent the crust from shrinking.
While the crust is chilling, preheat oven to 375 F (190°C).

Line the chilled pie crust with parchment paper. (Crunch up the parchment paper first so that you can easily shape it into the crust.) Fill with pie weights or dried beans. (Note that you will need at least 2 standard sets of pie weights to fit.) I like to push the weights up against the sides of the pie crust to help ensure the sides don’t shrink down.
Bake until the edges of the crust are starting to brown, about 15–16 minutes.
Remove pie from the oven and carefully lift the parchment paper (with the weights) out of the pie.
Prick holes all over the bottom crust with a fork.
If desired for a shiny golden crust, brush egg wash on edges.
Return pie crust to the oven and bake for 14–15 minutes, or until bottom has browned.
Remove from the oven, and cool on a wire rack completely before filling.
You can blind bake the crust up to 3 days ahead of time.
Cover cooled crust tightly and refrigerate until ready to fill. (Review this how to par-bake pie crust page if you need extra help with this blind-baking step.)
Whisk the egg yolks and cornstarch together. (A fork works nicely for this, or a small whisk.) Set aside.
Combine the milk, heavy cream, sugar, and salt in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Whisk until all the sugar has dissolved, then bring to a gentle simmer, whisking occasionally.
Once simmering, remove about 1/2 cup (120ml) of the milk mixture and, in a slow and steady stream, whisk into the egg yolk and cornstarch mixture.
Keep those egg yolks moving so they don’t scramble.
In a slow and steady stream, pour and whisk the egg yolk mixture into the pot.
The pudding will immediately begin to bubble and thicken.
Stand back and use caution as the bubbles may burst.
Cook for 1 full minute, whisking constantly.

Remove the pan from heat and whisk in the vanilla and butter. You will have about 3 cups of filling. If you notice a lot of little chunks of egg yolk, they may not have been tempered properly. To fix, you can run the warm custard through a fine mesh sieve.

Place a piece of plastic wrap directly on top of the warm pudding to prevent a skin from forming on top. Allow to cool for 15 minutes. I do not recommend any longer than this, as it will be too thick to spread neatly into the crust.
Slice 2–3 bananas and arrange slices into the cooled pie shell.
Sprinkle with cinnamon, if desired.
Spread custard on top (it’s ok if it is still slightly warm).
An offset spatula is helpful for spreading.
Place a piece of plastic wrap directly on top of the custard; again, to prevent a skin from forming.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours and up to 1 day.
Using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment, whip the heavy cream, sugar, and vanilla extract on medium-high speed until medium peaks form, about 3–4 minutes.
Medium peaks are between soft/loose peaks and stiff peaks and are the perfect consistency for topping and piping on desserts.

Pipe or spread the whipped cream on top. (I use Wilton 1M tip.) Slice remaining banana and garnish with banana slices and serve pie immediately, or refrigerate for up to 1 hour and then garnish with banana slices and serve. (Best to garnish with banana slices immediately before serving, but whipped cream can go on the pie up to 1 hour in advance.)
Slice and serve. Cover leftovers and store in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

title: CORN CHOWDER

ingredients.name: salt and ground black pepper to taste

steps: Cook bacon in a large pot over medium-high heat until crisp, 5 to 7 minutes; drain, leaving bacon and 2 tablespoons bacon fat in the pot.
Add potatoes and onion to the pot and cook about 5 minutes.
Pour in water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until potatoes are tender, about 20 minutes.
Stir in cream-style corn and half-and-half and heat through without boiling.
Season with salt and black pepper.

title: MANGO AND AVOCADO SALAD
steps: Whisk olive oil, lime juice, honey, cumin, chili powder, salt, and pepper until emulsified.
Add chopped mangoes, chopped avocados, diced red onion, and chopped cilantro to the bowl.
Toss gently to coat the fruit and vegetables with the dressing without crushing the avocado.

title: VEAL SHANK WITH NATURAL JUS

ingredients.name: veal shanks ((about 1 pound per shank), trimmed)
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

steps: Pat shanks dry with paper towel to remove moisture; using twine, circle circumference of each shank and tie tightly with twine
Season each shank with salt and freshly ground pepper; dredge each in flour, shaking off excess
In a large heavy pot, heat oil and butter until smoking; add tied veal shanks to hot pan
Brown all sides (3 minutes per side); remove browned shanks and reserve
Preheat oven to 300F
In the same pot, add onion, carrot and celery; season with salt to draw out moisture from vegetables
Saute until translucent; about 8 minutes
Add veal jus cubes until melted; combine
Add white wine and reduce liquid by half (5-8 minutes); add beef stock
Return shanks and juices to pan with stock; bring to boil
Place shanks, uncovered in oven for 3 hours; cooking liquid should be about 3/4 up each shank (add more stock, if necessary, or cover with foil or lid
Carefully remove cooked shanks; place in decorative serving platter; cut off and discard twine
Set pot with juices and sauce on stove; bring to a boil and reduce by 1/3 or ½
Sprinkle 1 tablespoon corn starch into sauce to thicken; stir constantly
Season with salt and pepper; pour over shanks and place remainder in pitcher alongside
Garnish with chopped parsley and lemon zest
Traditionally served over risotto; mashed potatoes also make a wonderful accompaniment

title: CHICKEN PARMESAN

ingredients.name: salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
panko bread crumbs, or more as needed
all-purpose flour, or more if needed
olive oil for frying, or as needed
fresh mozzarella, cut into small cubes

steps: Preheat oven to 450 F.
Pound chicken breasts between sheets of heavy plastic to 1/2 inch thick.
Season chicken with salt and pepper, then dust both sides with flour.
Beat eggs in a shallow bowl and combine panko with 1/2 cup Parmesan in another bowl.
Dip floured chicken in egg, press into the breadcrumb mixture on both sides, and rest 10 to 15 minutes.
Heat 1/2 inch olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and fry chicken in batches until golden, about 2 minutes per side.
Transfer chicken to a baking dish and top each breast with tomato sauce, mozzarella, basil, provolone, remaining Parmesan, and a drizzle of olive oil.
Bake 15 to 20 minutes until cheese is browned and bubbly and chicken reaches 165 F; rest briefly.

title: SEARED SCALLOPS WITH SQUASH PUREE

ingredients.name: grated Emporium Selection Premium Aged Cheddar

steps: Preheat your oven to 400 degrees F.
Place acorn squashes on your baking sheet.
Drizzle with a bit of oil and a few pinches of salt.
Place face side down.
Transfer to the oven to roast for 30 to 40 minutes, until tender.
Remove the squash from the oven and allow to cool for about 10 to 15 minutes, until they’re cool enough to handle.
Scoop the squash and add it to a food processor.
Remove the skin from the sweet potato and add the sweet potato to the food processor, along with the butter, garlic, cheddar and a few pinches of salt.
Process until mostly smooth (some texture is good!).
In a small pan, set over medium heat, add the walnuts and butter.
Toast them until they’re fragrant and slightly darker in color, about 2 minutes, until the butter is fragrant and turning a brown color.
Mix in the lemon juice, chives, a pinch of salt and pepper.
In a skillet, set over medium high heat, add the avocado oil.
When the oil is hot and glistening and glides along the pan smoothly, you know it’s hot enough.
Add the scallops and don’t move them until they begin to lift off the pan, about 2 minutes.
Flip the scallops and cook on their opposite side for 1 minute.

title: ROASTED ZUCCHINI CHUNKS
steps: Placeholder recipe created to link the current menu assignment. Replace with the approved production recipe before service.

title: ANCIENT GRAINS

ingredients.name: blend (e.g., farro, quinoa, spelt, or barley)

steps: Place the grain blend in a fine-mesh strainer. Rinse thoroughly under cold water to remove bitter saponins or excess starch.
Heat olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Saute the minced shallot and garlic for 2 minutes until soft and fragrant.
Add the rinsed grains to the pan. Stir continuously for 2 minutes to toast them lightly, enhancing their natural nutty flavor.
Pour in the broth and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes until the liquid is fully absorbed.
Remove the saucepan from the heat. Keep the lid on and let the grains steam undisturbed for 5 minutes.
Fluff the grains with a fork. Fold in the fresh parsley, chives, lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, and pepper. Serve warm.
notes: Placeholder only. Ingredients, quantities, and method still need chef review.

title: SMOKED SALMON CUCUMBER BITES
steps: Use a melon baller to scoop a 1/2-inch-deep cup from one side of each cucumber slice.
Place cucumber cups scooped-side down on paper towels and drain for 15 minutes.
Mix cream cheese, chopped dill, lemon zest, lemon juice, and pepper until well combined.
Spoon about 1/2 teaspoon dill cream into each cucumber cup.
Top each cup with a smoked salmon strip and a fresh dill sprig.

title: GAZPACHO SOUP

ingredients.name: red bell peppers, roasted, peeled, and seeded
English cucumber, peeled and roughly chopped
cold water (or more, for desired consistency)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
Garnish: Diced avocado, chopped cilantro, a drizzle of olive oil

steps: Use jarred roasted red peppers or roast your own and allow them to cool completely.
Peel and chop the cucumber and red onion. Peel the garlic clove.
Combine the roasted red peppers, chopped cucumber, red onion, garlic, olive oil, sherry vinegar, and cumin in a high-speed blender.
Blend on high until the mixture is completely smooth and silky.
With the blender running on low, slowly stream in the cold water until you reach your desired consistency.
Transfer the gazpacho to a container and chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour, or until thoroughly cold.

title: TURKEY ROULADE WITH SPINACH&SUNDRIED TOMATO                     (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: salt and freshly ground black pepper

steps: Butterfly the turkey breast and pound it to an even thickness.
Season the turkey with salt and pepper.
Warm olive oil in a skillet and cook garlic briefly, then wilt the spinach.
Spread spinach, chopped sun-dried tomatoes, and feta over the turkey.
Roll the turkey tightly around the filling and tie with kitchen twine.
Roast until the roulade reaches 165 F at the center.
Rest the roulade before slicing.

title: SPAGHETTI AND MEATBALLS IN MARINARA
steps: In a Dutch oven or large pan, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add onion and saute for a few minutes until softened and fragrant.
Add garlic and saute another minute.
In a medium saucepan, combine all sauce ingredients and mix well.
CHEF'S TIP: For chunky sauce, add all sauce ingredients to the onion and garlic.
If you prefer smooth sauce, place all ingredients PLUS the garlic and onion mixture into a blender and process until smooth.
Return mixture to the pan.Bring to a gentle boil over medium-high heat.
Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season to taste with garlic salt.
In a large bowl using a fork, combine all of the meatball ingredients.
Gently mix with your fork so as not to smash the ingredients together.
Too much working of the mixture can cause your meatballs to turn out tough.
Once everything is mixed, use your hands to be sure everything is combined well.
Work with hands for no longer than one minute.
Just long enough for everything to come together.
Use a cookie scoop to create meatballs.
I don’t roll mine, just place them straight from the cookie scoop into the pan.
You can roll them in your palms first, but again, only for a moment, don’t overwork the meat!
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat in large saute pan or skillet.
Brown meatballs on both sides, creating a nice crust.
Remove meatballs and drain fat.
Don’t scrape out any bits of meat from the pan!CHEF'S TIP: This makes a lot of meatballs.
I usually only cook about 10-12 in the marinara, and freeze the rest of the browned meatballs.
Deglaze the pan with 2 tablespoons of wine.
Return meatballs to the pan, pour marinara over the meatballs and bring to a gentle boil over medium-high heat.
Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, for a minimum of 30 minutes.
You can simmer the meatballs for up to 2 hours if you like, but you may need to add a little water throughout the process to keep the marinara from thickening too much.
Serve over cooked spaghetti noodles.

title: GRILLED TILAPIA WITH BALSAMIC ONION SAUCE

ingredients.name: tilapia fillets (about 6 ounces each), patted dry

steps: Rub tilapia fillets with 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Season both sides evenly with garlic powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add sliced red onions and a pinch of salt. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes until soft and deeply browned.

Pour the balsamic vinegar, broth, and honey into the skillet with the onions. Bring to a simmer. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook for 5 minutes until the liquid reduces by half and thickens into a glaze. Stir in cold butter if using, then remove from heat.

Preheat your grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Oil the grates well. Grill the tilapia fillets for 3 to 4 minutes per side. The fish is done when it flakes easily with a fork and reaches an internal temperature of 145eF (63eC).
Plate the grilled tilapia directly next to or on top of your herbed ancient grains. Spoon the warm balsamic onion sauce generously over the fish. Garnish with fresh parsley.
notes: Placeholder only. Ingredients, quantities, and method still need chef review.

title: BROCCOLINI WITH GARLIC CONFIT

ingredients.name: broccolini (ends trimmed, thick stalks split lengthwise)
pre-made garlic confit cloves plus 2 tbsp of the confit oil
Flaky sea salt and red pepper flakes (to taste)""

steps: Rinse the broccolini and shake off excess water. Cut any unusually thick stalks in half lengthwise so all pieces cook evenly.
Warm 2 tablespoons of the garlic confit oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the broccolini to the pan in a single layer. Let it cook undisturbed for 2 to 3 minutes until the underside starts to develop bright green and slightly charred spots.
Pour 2 tablespoons of water into the skillet. Immediately cover with a tight lid to trap the steam. Cook for 3 minutes until the stalks are tender-crisp.
Remove the lid and turn the heat down to low. Add the 1/2 cup of garlic confit cloves. Use a spoon to gently smash the soft garlic cloves into the oil, coating the broccolini.
Toss everything together for 1 minute until heated through. Remove from heat, squeeze the fresh lemon juice over the top, and season with flaky sea salt and a pinch of red pepper flakes.
notes: Placeholder only. Ingredients, quantities, and method still need chef review.

title: SUMAC SPICED ROASTED CARROT CHUNKS

ingredients.name: carrots (peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks)Oil: 2 tbsp olive oilPrimary Spice: 1.5 tbsp ground sumac

steps: Set your oven to 425eF (220eC) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Cut the carrots into uniform 1-inch chunks so they roast evenly.
Place the carrot chunks in a large mixing bowl. Drizzle with olive oil and toss until well coated. Add the sumac, garlic powder, cumin, salt, and pepper. Toss again to evenly distribute the spices.

Spread the carrots across the baking sheet in a single layer, ensuring they do not crowd each other. Roast for 20 to 25 minutes, tossing halfway through, until the edges are deeply caramelized and the centers are fork-tender.
Transfer the warm roasted carrots to your serving plate and garnish with fresh mint or parsley if desired.
notes: Placeholder only. Ingredients, quantities, and method still need chef review.

title: HERB AND CREAM CHEESE POLENTA
steps: Combine vegetable broth and milk in a medium saucepan. Bring to a gentle boil over medium-high heat.
Reduce heat to low. Slowly whisk in the cornmeal in a steady stream to prevent lumps.
Cook for 20 to 25 minutes, stirring frequently with a wooden spoon. The polenta is ready when it is thick, smooth, and pulls away slightly from the sides of the pan.
Remove from heat. Stir in the cream cheese and butter until fully melted and completely incorporated.
Add the chives, thyme, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Stir well to distribute the fresh herbs. Serve immediately while hot and creamy.
notes: Placeholder only. Ingredients, quantities, and method still need chef review.

title: LEMON BREAD WITH ICING
steps: Set your oven to 350eF (175eC). Grease an 8.5 x 4.5-inch loaf pan and line it with parchment paper.
Combine the flour, baking powder, and salt in a medium bowl. Set aside.
Rub the lemon zest into the granulated sugar in a large bowl to release the citrus oils. Whisk in the eggs, vegetable oil, sour cream, lemon juice, and vanilla extract until completely smooth.
Fold the dry ingredients into the wet mixture using a spatula just until combined. Do not overmix.
Pour the batter into the prepared loaf pan. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let cool completely.
Whisk the powdered sugar and lemon juice together until smooth. Drizzle over the cooled cake before slicing.
notes: Placeholder only. Ingredients, quantities, and method still need chef review.

title: GRILLED ZUCCHINI ROLLUP WITH RICOTTA
steps: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Trim zucchini ends and slice zucchini lengthwise into 1/4-inch-thick strips.
Brush zucchini slices lightly with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill slices in a single layer about 2 minutes per side until pliable with light grill marks, then cool on a baking sheet.
Combine ricotta, chopped basil, mint, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and freshly ground black pepper.
Spread a thin layer of ricotta mixture across each zucchini strip.
Place a whole basil leaf at one end of each strip, roll from that end, and repeat with remaining slices.

title: BACON AND CLAM FLATBREAD

ingredients.name: (10 oz) can Chicken of the Sea® Whole Baby Clams, rinsed and drained

steps: Preheat oven to 475 F.
Cover a baking sheet pan with parchment paper or foil and set aside.
Place butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add flour and cook, whisking constantly for 1 minute.
Add milk slowly, whisking constantly to keep lumps from forming, and cook until mixture has thickened slightly.
Remove from heat and stir in Parmesan cheese.
Season with salt and pepper.
Spread sauce over naan.
Arrange clams over sauce.
Sprinkle the Mozzarella or Cheddar, herbs de Provence and bacon over the top.
Bake until cheese is bubbly, about 8 minutes.
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste.

title: RED WINE BRAISED BEEF BRISKET
steps: Preheat oven to 350 F.
Mix thyme, salt, and black pepper, then rub the seasoning over both sides of the brisket.
Heat olive oil in a roasting pan over medium-high heat and brown brisket 3 to 4 minutes per side.
Remove brisket and cook sliced red onion in the hot pan about 2 minutes until slightly softened.
Stir in beef broth, tomato sauce, and red wine.
Return brisket to the roasting pan and cover with foil.
Roast 1 hour, baste with pan juices, cover again, and continue roasting 1 1/2 to 2 hours until very tender and the pan sauce thickens.

title: ROASTED PORK TENDERLOIN WITH OLIVE AND FETA SALSA

ingredients.name: large pork tenderloin (about 1 to 1.5 lbs), trimmed of silver skin
ripe Roma tomato (seeded and finely diced)

steps: Preheat your oven to 400eF (204eC). Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large, oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Rub the pork tenderloin with oregano, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Sear the pork in the hot skillet for 2 minutes on all sides until a golden-brown crust forms.
Transfer the skillet directly into the oven. Roast for 15 to 20 minutes until a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part reads 145eF (63eC).
Remove the pork from the oven, transfer it to a cutting board, and tent loosely with foil. Let it rest for 5 to 10 minutes to lock in the juices before slicing.
While the pork rests, toss the feta, olives, tomato, red onion, extra-virgin olive oil, lemon juice, and parsley together in a small bowl.
Slice the pork tenderloin into 1-inch thick medallions and spoon the cold olive and feta salsa generously over the warm meat.
notes: Placeholder only. Ingredients, quantities, and method still need chef review.

title: WILD MUSHROOM TART WITH GOAT CHEESE

ingredients.name: mushrooms (cleaned and stem cut off at cap base, finely sliced)

steps: Preheat the oven to 400F.
Mix goat cheese with honey, olive oil, thyme chopped leaves, salt, and pepper until creamy and spreadable. Set aside.
In a large skillet add the 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the shallots and mushrooms.
Sauté until all liquid has evaporated and the mushrooms starting to turn golden at the edges, 5 minutes.
Add the brandy and cook until evaporated, less than 1 minute.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Defrost the puff pastry and roll it out on a floured surface until it is half the thickness that you started with.
With a sharp knife cut 1 inch off from each side.
With a small baking brush, brush the egg mixture at the outer edges of the pastry.
Place the 1-inch strip of puff pastry over it to form a riser border.
The outer edges must be aligned one on top of the other properly.

Place the puff pastry over a silpat or parchment paper in a cookie sheet. Spread the goat cheese inside the puff pastry and top with the mushrooms. Spread some of the egg wash over the puff pastry border.
Bake in the warm oven for 20 minutes. Let it rest for a couple of minutes and serve.

title: ROASTED MEXICAN STREET CORN
steps: Preheat the oven to 425℉.
Slice the butter until several pats and dot them around a sheet pan. Place the pan in the oven to melt the butter.
When the butter is starting to brown, add the corn and toss well to coat.
Place the sheet pan back into the oven and cook for 6-8 minutes.
Remove from the oven and drizzle with Mexican crema and sprinkle with chili powder and cotija cheese. Top with chopped cilantro if desired.

title: BRAISED BEAN CASSOULET

ingredients.name: (15 oz each) Cannellini or Great Northern beans (rinsed and drained)

steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat. Add the onion, carrots, and celery. Cook for 8 minutes until soft.
Stir in the minced garlic and tomato paste. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the paste turns a deep brick-red color.
Pour in the white wine, using a wooden spoon to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Simmer for 3 minutes until the liquid reduces by half.
Add the rinsed beans, vegetable broth, diced tomatoes, thyme, rosemary, and the bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 25 minutes until the sauce thickens.
While simmering, mix the panko breadcrumbs with 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a small bowl. Toast in a separate skillet over medium heat for 3 minutes until golden brown.
Remove the herb sprigs and bay leaf from the cassoulet. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Stir in the fresh parsley and top with the toasted breadcrumbs just before serving.
notes: Placeholder only. Ingredients, quantities, and method still need chef review.

title: HERB ROASTED POTATO WEDGES
steps: Placeholder recipe created to link the current menu assignment. Replace with the approved production recipe before service.
notes: Placeholder only. Ingredients, quantities, and method still need chef review.

title: KEY LIME PIE

ingredients.name: garnish: lime zest, quartered lime slices, macadamia nuts, whipped cream, or meringue topping

steps: Preheat oven to 350 F (177°C).
Using a food processor, pulse the graham crackers and macadamia nuts together into coarse crumbs.
A few larger pieces of nuts is OK.
Pour into a medium bowl and stir in the sugar.
Add the melted butter and stir until combined.
The mixture will be thick, coarse, and sandy.
Try to smash/break up any large chunks.
Pour the mixture into an ungreased 9-inch pie dish.
Use your hand to pat down the crumbs into the bottom and up the sides to make a crust.
Use medium pressure—simply pat down until the mixture is no longer crumbly.
Tips: You can use a small flat-bottomed measuring cup to help press down the bottom crust and smooth out the surface, but do not pack down too hard.
And run a spoon around the bottom “corner” where the edge and bottom meet to help make a rounded crust⁠—this helps prevent the crust from falling apart when you slice into the pie.
For more shaping technique tips, see the graham cracker crust recipe page.
Pre-bake crust for 8 minutes. Remove from the oven and leave the oven on.
Whisk the sweetened condensed milk, lime juice, and egg yolks together (or use an electric mixer). Whisk in the lime zest. Pour into warm crust.

Bake the pie for 18–20 minutes or until only *slightly* jiggly in the center. You want it mostly set. Remove from the oven and allow to cool completely on a wire rack. Once cool, cover and chill for at least 1 hour (and up to 3 days) before serving.
Garnish as desired.

title: ZUCCHINI FRITTERS WITH DIP

ingredients.name: chives or scallions (chopped, green parts only)
Greek yogurt or sour cream (plain, full-fat )
clove (small, minced or grated with a microplane grater)
chives or scallions (chopped, green parts only)
chopped assorted fresh herbs (basil, lemon thyme, dill, tarragon, parsley, chervil, etc.)

steps: Grate the zucchini using either the grating attachment on a food processor or the large holes on a box grater.
Sprinkle the zucchini with 1 teaspoon of salt, gently mix, then spread out onto a large kitchen towel and let sit for about 20 minutes to release its liquid.
Fold up the towel and wring it our over a bowl or sink to extract the liquid. Be aggressive and really squeeze out as much liquid as your can.
In a medium bowl, mix together the chives or scallions, egg, flour, corn starch, baking powder, pepper and the remaining ½ teaspoon of salt.
Add the zucchini and mix until everything is thoroughly combined.
Heat about a ¼- ½ inch of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, then line a baking sheet with paper towels and set it aside nearby.

Carefully drop heaping spoonfuls of the zucchini mixture into the hot oil and lightly press with the back of the spoon to flatten. Be careful not to overcrowd the pan. About 4 per batch is usually just right.
Cook until the edges are golden, about 3 to 4 minutes. Flip the fritters and fry them on the other side for another 2-3 minutes, until nicely browned, then remove to the paper towels to drain.
To make the sauce, add the yogurt, garlic, lemon juice, salt, pepper, chives or scallions and herbs in a small bowl and mix to combine. Taste, and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the fritters while they're hot with the lemon herb yogurt on the side.
To serve later, place the fritters in a 200 ℉ oven until ready to serve.

title: SOBA NOODLE SALAD

ingredients.name: Juice of half a lime ((full lime if the lime is small or less juicy))
sesame oil (, plus more for coating noodles)
soy sauce ((use tamari for gluten-free, coconut aminos for soyfree))
asian chile sauce (sambal oelek) (or sriracha or other hot sauce to taste)
Soba noodles (, use pure buckwheat soba or rice noodles for gluten-free)
Sesame seeds (, green onion, cilantro/mint (optional), crushed roasted peanuts/cashews(optional) for garnish)
optional additions: edamame (, baked tofu, other crunchy veggies etc.)

steps: Cook the noodles according to instruction on the package. (Simmer in boiling water for 4 to 5 mins for the wheat + buckwheat soba). Drain and rinse with cold water.
Add the noodles to a bowl and add a tsp of oil or sesame oil and toss to coat.
Add the veggies and half of the green onions to the bowl.
Add the dressing to the bowl and toss well.
Garnish with green onions, crushed nuts(optional) and sesame seeds (optional). Chill and serve.

title: SHRIMP LINGUINI WITH SAFFRON SAUCE AND PEPPERED GREENS

ingredients.name: (450g) large shrimp (peeled, deveined, and tails removed)

steps: Crush the saffron threads slightly with your fingers into a small bowl. Pour the 3 tablespoons of warm broth over them. Let sit for 10 minutes to draw out the deep golden color and aroma.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the linguini according to package instructions until al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup of pasta water, then drain the noodles.

Heat 1 tablespoon each of butter and olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season shrimp lightly with salt. Sear for 1 to 2 minutes per side until pink and opaque. Remove shrimp from the skillet and set aside.
Turn heat down to medium. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter to the same skillet. Saute the minced shallot and garlic for 2 minutes until soft.

Pour in the white wine, scraping up the browned bits from the bottom. Simmer for 3 minutes until reduced by half. Stir in the heavy cream and the bloomed saffron liquid (including the threads). Simmer gently for 4 minutes until the sauce turns bright yellow and thickens slightly.

Add the cooked linguini and seared shrimp into the skillet. Toss continuously for 1 to 2 minutes until the pasta is coated in the golden sauce, adding a splash of reserved pasta water if the sauce gets too thick. Stir in the Parmigiano-Reggiano.
In a quick bowl, toss the fresh arugula with the extra-virgin olive oil, lemon juice, and a heavy pinch of freshly cracked black pepper.
Plate a swirl of the golden saffron linguini and shrimp. Top each portion with a loose handful of the peppered arugula just before serving.

title: ROASTED CHICKEN BREAST IN CREAMY SUNDRIED TOMATO SAUCE

ingredients.name: / 1.5lb chicken thighs OR chicken breast (, skinless and boneless)
sun dried tomatoes (, strips, drained of oil (Note 1))
oil from sun dried tomatoes jar ((Note 1))

steps: Breast: Cut chicken in half horiztonally to form 2 thin steaks.
Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper. Heat oil from sun dried tomat jar in a large skillet over high heat.
Add the chicken and cook until golden brown on both sides - 6 minutes total for thigh or 4 minutes for breast. Remove onto a plate, keep warm.
Add the garlic and cook for 15 seconds until light golden.
Add wine. Bring to simmer and scrape the bottom of the skillet to mix all the brown bits into the wine.
Let the wine simmer for 1 minute or so until the smell of alcohol is gone which means it has evaporated.
Mix in mustard, then add the chicken broth, cream, parmesan and sun dried tomatoes.
Bring to simmer, then reduce heat to medium and simmer for 2 to 3 minutes until thickened. Stir regularly to dissolve parmesan cheese.
Taste sauce and add more salt and pepper if needed.
Stir through basil leaves, then return chicken into pan. Spoon sauce over and simmer 1 minute.

title: SEARED SALMON WITH BASIL PESTO SAUCE

ingredients.name: crushed red pepper flakes (I use a generous pinch)
kosher salt & freshly ground black pepper

steps: Heat a dry skillet, over medium heat, and lightly toast pine nuts (gently toss and shake pan occasionally); then place in a food processor.
To the food processor, add basil and garlic; pulse just to combine.
With processor running, drizzle in olive oil, and process, scraping down sides once or twice with a rubber spatula, to desired consistency (I like it sort of chunky).
Add in Parmesan cheese, red pepper flakes, lemon juice, and salt and pepper to taste; pulse to combine; set aside.
Wipe the salmon dry with paper towels (to allow a nice sear), then season lightly with salt and pepper.
Heat the skillet, over medium-high heat, then spray with cooking spray.
Cook salmon about 4 minutes per side or to desired doneness (I like it a little pinkish in the center since it will continue cooking a little when removed from the pan).
Serve salmon with pesto spooned over top.
Can also be served at room temperature.

title: PEACH COBBLER

ingredients.name: peaches, peeled, pitted, and sliced into thin wedges
unsalted butter, chilled and cut into small pieces

steps: Preheat oven to 425 F.
Toss peaches with 1/4 cup white sugar, 1/4 cup brown sugar, 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon, nutmeg, lemon juice, and cornstarch.
Pour peach mixture into a 2-quart baking dish and bake 10 minutes.
Combine flour, 1/4 cup white sugar, 1/4 cup brown sugar, baking powder, and salt.
Cut in chilled butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs, then stir in boiling water just until combined.
Remove peaches from the oven and drop spoonfuls of topping over them.
Mix 3 tablespoons white sugar with 1 teaspoon cinnamon and sprinkle over the cobbler.
Bake about 30 minutes until the topping is golden.
