title: MISO & SHRIMIP SOUP WITH SCALLION DUMPLING

ingredients.name: pre -made scallion or vegetable dumplings (frozen is fine)

steps: Heat dashi stock or broth in a large pot over medium heat. Stir in the grated ginger and minced garlic. Bring to a gentle simmer for 5 minutes to infuse the flavors.
Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms and baby bok choy to the simmering broth. Cook for 3 minutes until the vegetables begin to soften.

Drop the scallion dumplings and shrimp directly into the pot. Simmer gently for 4 to 5 minutes until the dumplings are cooked through (floating) and the shrimp turn pink and opaque. Turn off the heat.
Temper the miso paste with hot broth and whisk until completely smooth and lump-free. Pour this mixture back into the pot and stir gently to combine.
Stir in the toasted sesame oil. Ladle the soup evenly into deep bowls, ensuring each portion gets 3 dumplings and plenty of shrimp. Top generously with the sliced fresh scallions.

title: GINGER SOY SALMON WITH JASMINE RICE
steps: Rinse the Jasmine rice thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Combine the rinsed rice, water, and salt in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then cover with a tight lid, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 15 minutes. Remove from heat and let steam covered for 5 minutes before fluffing.
Whisk together the soy sauce, honey, sugar, grated ginger, minced garlic, rice vinegar, and toasted sesame oil until the honey dissolves.
Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Place the salmon fillets skin-side up (flesh-side down) in the pan. Sear undisturbed for 4 minutes until a golden crust forms.
Flip the salmon over. Pour the ginger soy sauce mixture directly into the pan around the fish.

Lower the heat to medium. Simmer for 3 to 4 minutes while continuously spooning the bubbling sauce over the tops of the fillets. The sauce will quickly thicken into a glossy glaze, and the salmon will flake easily when done.
Spoon a generous base of fluffy Jasmine rice into bowls. Place a glazed salmon fillet on top, drizzle with extra pan sauce, and garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds.

title: VEGETABLE LASAGNA

ingredients.name: lasagna noodles (oven-ready or boiled al dente)

steps: Sauté the vegetables: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the onion, zucchini, mushrooms, and bell pepper. Sauté for 6 to 8 minutes until tender and their moisture has evaporated. Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 minute. Add the spinach, tossing until completely wilted. Remove from heat.
Stir the ricotta cheese, egg, grated parmesan, dried oregano, salt, and pepper together in a medium bowl until uniform.

Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Spread 1 cup of marinara sauce across the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish. Lay 4 lasagna noodles over the sauce. Spread half of the ricotta mixture over the noodles, top with half of the cooked vegetable filling, drizzle with 1 cup of marinara, and sprinkle with 1 cup of mozzarella.

Add another layer of 4 noodles, the remaining ricotta mixture, the remaining vegetables, another cup of sauce, and 1 cup of mozzarella. Top with the final 4 noodles, the remaining marinara sauce, and the final cup of mozzarella cheese.

Cover the baking dish tightly with aluminum foil (foil should not touch the cheese). Bake for 25 minutes. Remove the foil and bake uncovered for an additional 15 to 20 minutes until the cheese is bubbly and golden brown.
Let the lasagna rest undisturbed for 15 minutes before slicing so the layers hold their shape.

title: MEDITERRANEAN TOMATO, OLIVE, & FETA IN GREEK DRESSING
steps: Combine the extra-virgin olive oil, red wine vinegar, minced garlic, dried oregano, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper in a small bowl or jar. Whisk vigorously or shake well until the dressing emulsifies.
Core and chop the tomatoes into bite-sized chunks. Slice the cucumber and thinly slice the red onion. Place the chopped tomatoes, cucumbers, and onions into a large mixing bowl.
Toss the halved Kalamata olives into the bowl with the vegetables. Gently fold in the cubed or crumbled feta cheese.
Pour the Greek dressing over the salad components. Use a large spoon or salad tongs to toss gently, ensuring everything is evenly coated without breaking up the feta cheese too much.
Let the salad sit at room temperature for 10 minutes before serving. This allows the tomatoes to release their natural juices and mingle with the vinaigrette.

title: MANGO PUDDING

ingredients.name: fresh mango puree (from about 2-3 sweet, ripe mangoes like Ataulfo)
whole milk (or coconut milk for a tropical twist)

steps: Pour the 1/2 cup of hot water (just below boiling) into a medium bowl. Sprinkle the gelatin powder evenly over the top. Stir vigorously with a fork or small whisk for 1 to 2 minutes until completely dissolved and clear.
Stir the granulated sugar into the warm gelatin mixture until the grains completely dissolve.
In a large bowl or blender, combine the smooth mango puree, milk, and the sweet gelatin mixture. Blend or whisk thoroughly until the color is completely uniform and smooth.
Pour the liquid mixture evenly into dessert bowls, ramekins, or glasses. Chill for at least 3 hours until fully set and firm to a gentle touch.
Just before serving, drizzle a tablespoon of cold evaporated milk over the top of each pudding. Crown with a small pile of fresh mango chunks.

title: ROASTED RED PEPPER WITH DILL YOGURT

ingredients.name: large red bell peppers (halved and seeded)
salt, and black pepperDill Yogurt Swirl: 1/2 cup plain Greek yogurt
fresh lemon juice, a pinch of salt

steps: Place pepper halves cut-side down on a foil-lined baking sheet. Roast under a high oven broil for 15 to 20 minutes until the skins are blackened. Transfer to a bowl, cover with plastic wrap for 10 minutes, then peel off and discard the skins. Roughly chop the flesh.Sauté aromatics: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion and garlic for 5 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add the chopped roasted peppers, vegetable broth, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes.
Use an immersion blender or stand blender to puree the soup until completely smooth and velvety.
In a small bowl, stir together the Greek yogurt, fresh dill, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Spoon a generous dollop of the dill yogurt into the center of each bowl and swirl it through the red soup.

title: TURKEY MEDALLION WITH CRANBERRY ORANGE CHUTNEY

ingredients.name: turkey breast tenderloin (cut into 1-inch thick medallions)Turkey Seasoning: 1 tbsp olive oil
salt, and black pepperChutney Base: 2 cups fresh or frozen cranberries
medium orange (zested and juiced)Chutney Sweetener & Spice: 1/3 cup honey or maple syrup

steps: Combine cranberries, orange zest, orange juice, honey, apple cider vinegar, red onion, grated ginger, and cinnamon in a medium saucepan over medium heat.

Bring the chutney mixture to a gentle boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer uncovered for 10 to 12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cranberries burst and the sauce thickens into a glossy glaze. Remove from heat.
Pat the turkey medallions dry with paper towels. Rub both sides evenly with olive oil, poultry seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the turkey medallions to the pan in a single layer. Cook undisturbed for 4 to 5 minutes until a golden crust forms.
Flip the medallions over. Reduce heat to medium and cook for another 4 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) on a meat thermometer. Transfer to a plate to rest for 3 minutes.
Arrange the warm turkey medallions on serving plates and spoon the vibrant cranberry orange chutney generously across the top.

title: LOBSTER / SHRIMP ROLL ON TOASTED BRIOCHE

ingredients.name: cooked lobster meat (chopped) and 1/2 lb cooked shrimp (peeled, deveined, and chopped)The
finely diced celery (for crunch)Herbs & Seasoning: 2 tbsp finely chopped fresh chives
salt, and black pepper (to taste)The Vessel: 4 top-split brioche hot dog buns

steps: Ensure the cooked lobster and shrimp are thoroughly chilled. Chop them into bite-sized chunks and pat dry with paper towels to prevent a watery dressing.

In a large bowl, whisk together the mayonnaise, fresh lemon juice, Old Bay seasoning, salt, and pepper. Fold in the chopped celery, chives, lobster, and shrimp until evenly coated. Hold chilled while prepping the bread.
Heat a skillet or griddle over medium heat. Spread the softened butter generously onto both outer sides of the top-split brioche buns.
Place the buns in the hot skillet. Toast for 2 minutes per side until the edges are golden brown, crispy, and warm.
Carefully open the warm brioche buns. Spoon the chilled lobster and shrimp salad generously into each toasted roll. Garnish with a tiny pinch of extra chives or Old Bay on top.

title: ARUGULA & PEAR WITH BALSAMIC VINAIGRETTE

ingredients.name: ripe Bosc or Anjou pears (thinly sliced)
toasted walnuts or pecans [1, 3]Balsamic Vinaigrette: 1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil
salt, and freshly cracked black pepper (to taste) [1

steps: Combine the extra-virgin olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper. Whisk vigorously until emulsified.
If the walnuts are not toasted, toss them in a dry skillet over medium heat for 3 to 4 minutes until fragrant, then let them cool completely.
Place the fresh baby arugula into a large salad bowl.
Arrange the thin pear slices evenly over the arugula bed. Top with the crumbled cheese and cooled toasted walnuts.
Drizzle the balsamic vinaigrette over the salad just before serving, tossing gently to coat the delicate leaves without bruising the pears.

title: LEMON SORBET

ingredients.name: freshly squeezed lemon juice (from about 5 to 6 large juicy lemons)The Sweetener: 1 cup granulated sugar
waterBrightener: 1 tbsp finely grated lemon zest

steps: Combine the water and granulated sugar in a small saucepan over medium heat. Stir frequently for 3 to 5 minutes until the sugar dissolves completely and the liquid turns clear. Do not let it boil vigorously. Remove from heat.
Stir the fresh lemon zest directly into the hot simple syrup. Let the syrup cool completely to room temperature, allowing the oils from the zest to steep into the liquid.

Pour the freshly squeezed lemon juice into the cooled sugar syrup and stir thoroughly to combine. For an ultra-smooth texture, pour the mixture through a fine-mesh strainer into a bowl to remove any remaining pulp or zest pieces.

Chill the mixture for at least 1 hour until completely cold. Churn the chilled liquid in an ice cream maker until it reaches a soft slushy consistency, then transfer to an airtight container and freeze for 2 to 3 hours until firm. For no-churn production, pour the cold liquid into a shallow metal baking dish and freeze, stirring every 30 minutes for the first 2 to 3 hours until the texture is uniformly icy and scoopable.

title: CHICKEN TORTILLA

ingredients.name: (14 oz) crushed tomatoesFillings: 3 cups cooked shredded chicken (rotisserie chicken works perfectly)
frozen or canned cornBrightener: 2 tbsp fresh lemon or lime juiceToppings: 1 cup crushed tortilla chips or fried tortilla strips
crumbled cotija or feta cheese, fresh cilantro leaves

steps: Sauté aromatics: Heat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion and minced jalapeño for 5 minutes until soft and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 additional minute until fragrant.
Stir the cumin, chili powder, dried oregano, and salt directly into the onion mixture. Let the spices toast in the dry heat for 30 seconds to fully release their essential oils.
Pour in the chicken broth and crushed tomatoes. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil, then turn the heat down to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer gently for 10 minutes to marry the flavors.
Stir in the shredded chicken, rinsed black beans, and corn. Simmer uncovered for 5 minutes until the fillings are heated completely through.
Turn off the heat and stir in the fresh lemon or lime juice to brighten the rich broth. Taste and add extra salt if desired.

Ladle the hot soup into deep bowls. Pile the toppings generously on top of each portion just before serving, starting with the crunchy tortilla chips, followed by avocado chunks, crumbled cheese, and cilantro.

title: GRILLED FISH WITH SALSA

ingredients.name: firm white fish fillets (such as mahi-mahi, cod, halibut, or tilapia), patted dryFish Rub: 1 tbsp olive oil
salt, and black pepperFresh Salsa: 1 cup diced ripe mango or tomatoes
jalape ño (seeded and minced)Brightener: 2 tbsp fresh lime juice

steps: Toss together the selected salsa base (diced mango or tomatoes), red bell pepper, red onion, cilantro, and jalapeño. Drizzle with the lime juice, extra-virgin olive oil, and salt. Stir well and set aside at room temperature to let the flavors marry.
Rub both sides of the fish fillets with 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Season evenly with the chili powder, garlic powder, cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Heat the grill or grill pan over medium-high heat. Ensure the grates are completely clean and well-oiled to prevent sticking.

Place the fish fillets on the grill. Cook undisturbed for 3 to 4 minutes per side. The fish is ready when it develops nice grill marks, releases easily from the grates, and flakes easily with a fork (internal temperature of 145°F/63°C).
Transfer the grilled fish fillets to serving plates. Spoon the fresh salsa generously over the top of each fillet, allowing the vibrant juices to pool around the fish.

title: EGGPLANT PARMESAN

ingredients.name: salt (for sweating)Breading Station: 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
grated parmesan cheeseSauce & Cheese: 1 jar (24 oz) high-quality marinara sauce
fresh basil leaves (torn)Cooking: Olive oil spray

steps: Lay eggplant rounds on paper towels and sprinkle both sides with salt. Let sit for 20 minutes to draw out bitter moisture. Rinse thoroughly under cold water and pat completely dry.
Set oven to 425°F (220°C) and line two large baking sheets with parchment paper.

Set up three shallow bowls: one with flour, one with beaten eggs, and one mixing the panko crumbs and grated parmesan. Dip each eggplant slice into the flour, then the egg, and finally press firmly into the panko mixture to coat evenly.
Arrange the slices in a single layer on the baking sheets. Spray the tops lightly with olive oil spray. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, flipping halfway through, until golden-brown and crispy.

In an 8x11-inch baking dish, spread 1/2 cup of marinara sauce. Layer half of the baked eggplant slices over the sauce. Top with half of the remaining marinara, 1 cup of mozzarella, and a few torn basil leaves. Repeat with the remaining eggplant, sauce, mozzarella, and a final dust of parmesan.
Bake at 400°F (204°C) for 15 to 20 minutes until the cheese is melted, bubbly, and golden around the edges. Let rest for 10 minutes before slicing to allow the layers to set.

title: CORN & AVOCADO WITH HONEY MUSTARD VINAIGRETTE

ingredients.name: sweet corn (fresh shucked, canned, or thawed frozen)
finely diced red onionHoney Mustard Vinaigrette: 3 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil

steps: In a small bowl or jar, whisk together the olive oil, apple cider vinegar, honey, Dijon mustard, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper until completely smooth and emulsified.
Slice the cherry tomatoes in half and finely dice the red onion. Cube the avocados into bite-sized chunks.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the sweet corn, halved cherry tomatoes, and diced red onion.
Add the avocado chunks to the bowl last so they do not get mashed during mixing.
Pour the honey mustard vinaigrette over the ingredients. Use a rubber spatula or large spoon to gently fold the salad together until everything is evenly coated.

title: COCONUT FLAN

ingredients.name: waterThe Custard Base: 1 can (14 oz) sweetened condensed milk

steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Set a 9-inch round cake pan or ramekins inside a larger, high-sided roasting pan.

Combine sugar and water in a heavy saucepan over medium-high heat. Swirl the pan occasionally—do not stir—until the sugar melts and turns a deep amber color (about 6 to 8 minutes). Immediately pour the hot caramel into the bottom of the cake pan, swirling quickly to coat the bottom before it hardens.
In a large bowl or blender, combine the sweetened condensed milk, coconut milk, eggs, vanilla extract, and salt. Blend or whisk until completely smooth and uniform.
Pour the custard mixture through a fine-mesh strainer directly over the hardened caramel in the cake pan to ensure an ultra-smooth texture.

Bring a kettle of water to a boil. Pour the hot water into the outer roasting pan until it reaches halfway up the sides of the flan pan. This water bath (bain-marie) ensures even cooking without curdling.
Bake for 45 to 50 minutes. The flan is ready when the edges are set but the center still jiggles slightly like gelatin.
Remove the flan pan from the water bath and let it cool to room temperature. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours to fully set.

Run a thin knife around the edges of the pan. Place a large rimmed serving plate upside down over the pan, then quickly invert both. The flan will slide out, and the liquid caramel sauce will run down the sides. Top with toasted coconut.

title: TOMATO BASIL

ingredients.name: (28 oz each) whole peeled San Marzano tomatoes, with their juices
cloves garlic (minced)Liquids: 2 cups low-sodium vegetable or chicken broth
sugar (optional, to balance acidity)Seasoning: 1/2 tsp sea salt

steps: Sauté aromatics: Heat the butter and olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion for 5 minutes until soft and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 additional minute until fragrant.

Pour in the canned whole tomatoes with their juices, along with the broth. Use a wooden spoon to gently break apart the whole tomatoes. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat source. Add the fresh basil leaves directly into the hot liquid. Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until it is completely smooth and velvety.
Stir in the heavy cream and sugar (if using). Taste the broth and adjust with extra salt and pepper as needed.
Return the pot to low heat for 2 minutes just to warm everything through. Ladle into warm bowls and garnish with extra fresh basil leaves and a tiny drizzle of olive oil.

title: PHILLY CHEESESTEAK

ingredients.name: ribeye steak (very thinly shaved or sliced)

green bell pepper (thinly sliced, optional)The Cheese: 8 slices Provolone cheese (or 1/2 cup warm Cheez Whiz)The Bread: 4 soft Italian hoagie rolls (split lengthwise)Oil & Seasoning: 2 tbsp vegetable oil

steps: Place ribeye steak in the freezer for 20 to 30 minutes before prepping. This firms up the meat and makes it significantly easier to slice into paper-thin ribbons.Sauté the vegetables: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or flat-top griddle over medium-high heat. Cook the sliced onion and bell pepper for 6 to 8 minutes until soft and caramelized. Move them to the side of the pan.

Crank the heat up to high. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil to the empty side of the pan. Toss in the shaved ribeye, breaking it up with a spatula. Season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cook for 2 to 3 minutes until the meat is browned and no longer pink.
Toss the caramelized onions and peppers directly into the cooked beef. Divide the mixture into 4 oblong piles right in the skillet, matching the rough length of the hoagie rolls.
Lay 2 slices of Provolone cheese evenly over the top of each meat pile. Turn off the heat and let the steam melt the cheese completely (about 1 minute).
Open the hoagie rolls and place them face-down directly over the melted cheese piles. Slide a long spatula underneath the meat and lift, flipping the sandwich right-side up. Serve hot.

title: VEGETABLE & TOFU STIRFRY

ingredients.name: cornstarch (for thickening)Aromatics & Oil: 2 tbsp vegetable oil (divided)

steps: Press the block of tofu with paper towels and a heavy object for 15 minutes to remove excess water. Cut into 1-inch cubes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, water, honey, and cornstarch until the cornstarch completely dissolves. Set aside.

Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the tofu cubes in a single layer. Cook undisturbed for 3 to 4 minutes per side until golden-brown and crispy. Remove from the pan and set aside.Stir-fry the vegetables: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil to the same pan. Toss in the broccoli, bell pepper, snap peas, and carrot. Stir-fry vigorously for 4 to 5 minutes until the vegetables are tender-crisp and bright green.
Push the vegetables to the outer edges of the pan. Add the minced ginger and garlic to the center. Stir constantly for 30 seconds until highly fragrant.
Pour the stir-fry sauce mixture into the pan. Bring to a quick simmer; it will thicken into a glossy glaze within 1 minute. Return the crispy tofu to the pan and toss everything gently to coat.
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve hot over rice or noodles.

title: BEET & GOAT CHEESE WITH RED WINE VINAIGRETTE

ingredients.name: fresh baby spinach or mixed greens
toasted sliced almondsRed Wine Vinaigrette: 1/3 cup extra-virgin olive oil
salt, and freshly cracked black pepper (to taste

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (204°C). Trim the stems off the beets and wash them thoroughly. Wrap each beet tightly in aluminum foil and roast on a baking sheet for 45 minutes until a fork easily pierces the center. Let cool, rub the skins off with a paper towel, and slice into bite-sized wedges.
Combine the extra-virgin olive oil, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, honey, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl or jar. Whisk vigorously until the mixture emulsifies.
Arrange a bed of fresh baby spinach or mixed greens on a large serving platter or split into individual salad bowls.
Scatter the cooled, sliced beet wedges evenly across the greens. Top with the crumbled goat cheese and toasted sliced almonds.
Drizzle the red wine vinaigrette over the salad components just before serving, tossing gently to coat the leaves.

title: CHAI POACHED PEARS WITH CINNAMON TOPPING

ingredients.name: firm , ripe pears (such as Bosc or Anjou), peeled with stems left intactPoaching Liquid: 3 cups water
chai tea bags (or 2 tbsp loose-leaf chai blend)
heavy cream or plain Greek yogurt (for a dollop base)

steps: Bring the water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Remove from heat, drop in the chai tea bags, and let steep for 7 minutes. Remove and squeeze the tea bags to extract all the flavor.
Stir the sugar, cinnamon stick, star anise, and whole cloves into the hot chai tea. Bring the mixture back to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat until the sugar dissolves completely.

Slice a tiny sliver off the bottom of each peeled pear so they can stand upright. Submerge the pears into the simmering chai liquid. Cover the pan and cook for 20 to 25 minutes, turning them occasionally, until they are fork-tender but not mushy.

Lift the pears out of the liquid and set them aside on a plate. Crank the stove heat to high and boil the remaining chai liquid for 8 to 10 minutes until it reduces into a glossy, pourable syrup. Remove the whole spices.
Whip the heavy cream with the maple syrup and ground cinnamon until soft peaks form (or simply stir the cinnamon and maple syrup directly into Greek yogurt for a tangy spin).

Stand an upright poached pear in a shallow dessert bowl. Drizzle a generous spoonful of the warm, reduced chai syrup over the top. Add a dollop of the cinnamon cream to the side and scatter the toasted sliced almonds over the plate.

title: SEAFOOD CHOWDER

ingredients.name: firm white fish like cod or haddock (cut into 1-inch chunks)
all-purpose flourLiquids: 2 cups clam juice (or high-quality seafood broth)
heavy creamVegetables & Herbs: 2 medium Russet potatoes (peeled and diced into 1/2-inch cubes)
finely chopped fresh chives or parsley

steps: In a large Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat, cook the diced bacon until crispy (about 5 to 6 minutes). Use a slotted spoon to remove the bacon bits and set them aside, leaving the rendered fat in the pot.Sauté the aromatics: Add the butter to the bacon fat. Once melted, toss in the diced onion and celery. Sauté for 5 minutes until the vegetables are soft and translucent.
Sprinkle the flour evenly over the vegetables. Stir continuously for 2 minutes to cook out the raw flour taste; the mixture will form a thick paste.

Slowly pour in the clam juice while whisking vigorously to prevent lumps. Stir in the whole milk, heavy cream, diced potatoes, bay leaf, and thyme sprigs. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the potatoes are fork-tender.

Turn the heat down to low. Stir in the fish chunks, shrimp, and scallops. Simmer gently, uncovered, for 5 to 6 minutes. Do not boil, or the seafood will turn rubbery. The chowder is ready when the shrimp are pink and the fish flakes easily.

Remove the bay leaf and thyme sprigs. Taste and season with salt and black pepper. Ladle the hot chowder into bowls, top generously with the reserved crispy bacon bits, and scatter fresh chives over the top.

title: SPICED CHICKEN, BRIE, & AVOCADO MELT

ingredients.name: chicken breast (sliced horizontally into thin cutlets)Chicken Rub: 1 tbsp olive oil
salt, and black pepperThe Fillings: 1 large wheel of Brie cheese (sliced into 1/4-inch thick strips)
ripe avocados (sliced)The Greens & Crunch: 2 cups fresh baby arugula
soft artisan rolls (such as ciabatta, brioche, or panini buns)Spread: 4 tbsp mayonnaise or garlic aioli

steps: Pat the chicken cutlets dry with a paper towel. Rub both sides evenly with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, salt, and black pepper.

Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken cutlets in a single layer. Cook for 4 to 5 minutes per side until a golden crust forms and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). Remove from heat.
Split the artisan rolls lengthwise. Spread a layer of mayonnaise or garlic aioli across the inside surfaces of both the top and bottom buns.

Place the hot, cooked chicken cutlets directly onto the bottom buns. Layer the sliced Brie cheese immediately on top of the warm chicken so it begins to soften. Arrange the fresh avocado slices directly over the cheese.

Place the open-faced bottom halves under an oven broiler set to high for 1 to 2 minutes, or compress the assembled sandwiches in a preheated panini press until the bread turns golden-brown and the Brie is melted.
Top the melted cheese and avocado layer with a handful of fresh baby arugula. Close the sandwiches with the top buns, slice diagonally, and serve hot.

title: VEGETABLE PAELLA

ingredients.name: Bomba rice (or short-grain Calasparra / Arborio rice)
cloves garlic (minced)Flavor Builders: 1 large grated vine tomato (discard skin)
dry white wineLiquids: 3 1/2 cups low-sodium vegetable broth (warmed)

steps: Crush the saffron threads slightly and place them in a small cup. Pour 3 tablespoons of warm broth over them and let steep for 10 minutes to release its rich aroma and golden hue.Sauté the aromatics: Heat the olive oil in a traditional 12-to-14-inch paella pan (or a wide, shallow skillet) over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion, red bell pepper, and green bell pepper for 6 to 8 minutes until soft and slightly caramelized.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated tomato, and smoked paprika. Cook for 3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the mixture darkens into a thick, fragrant paste (the sofrito).
Pour in the white wine to deglaze the pan, scraping up any stuck bits. Add the Bomba rice and stir for 2 minutes, ensuring every grain is translucent and coated in the flavorful oil.

Pour in the warm vegetable broth, the bloomed saffron liquid, artichoke hearts, and green beans. Shake the pan gently to distribute the ingredients evenly. Do not stir the rice again from this point forward. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer uncovered for 15 to 18 minutes until the liquid is mostly absorbed.

Scatter the frozen peas over the top. Turn the heat up to medium-high for the last 2 minutes. Listen for a distinct crackling sound and smell a deeply toasted aroma—this forms the socarrat, the highly prized crispy rice crust at the bottom of the pan. Turn off the heat.
Cover the pan tightly with a clean kitchen towel or foil and let it rest undisturbed for 5 minutes. Garnish with fresh parsley and lemon wedges before serving directly from the pan.

title: CITRUS GREENS WITH CHOICE DRESSING

ingredients.name: fresh spring mix or baby spinach
mandarin oranges or clementines (peeled and separated into segments)
ruby red grapefruit (peeled and sliced into rounds)
shaved red onionOption A: Zesty Citrus-Herb Vinaigrette: 1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil
finely chopped fresh mint or parsley
salt, and black pepperOption B: Creamy Avocado-Lime

steps: Peel the citrus fruits completely, ensuring you remove as much of the bitter white pith as possible. Slice the grapefruit into thin wheels and separate the mandarins into clean segments.Whisk Option A (Vinaigrette): Combine all vinaigrette ingredients in a small jar. Shake vigorously for 30 seconds until the honey and citrus juices emulsify completely with the olive oil.Blend Option B (Creamy): Place the avocado, Greek yogurt, lime juice, cilantro, garlic, water, and salt into a small food processor or blender. Blend on high until completely smooth and velvety.
Arrange the salad greens across a wide serving platter. Scatter the sliced cucumber and shaved red onions evenly over the top.
Arrange the bright orange segments and pink grapefruit wheels over the bed of greens to create a visual centerpiece.
Keep the selected dressing on the side. Drizzle over individual portions just before eating to ensure the delicate salad leaves stay perfectly crisp.

title: KEY LIME MOUSSE

ingredients.name: The Citrus Base: 1/3 cup fresh Key lime juice (or standard lime juice)
Key lime zestThe Whip: 1 cup heavy whipping cream (cold)
graham crackers (crushed into crumbs), lime slices, or extra zest

steps: In a large bowl, whisk together the sweetened condensed milk, fresh Key lime juice, and lime zest. The acid from the lime juice will react with the condensed milk, causing it to thicken. Set aside.
In a separate chilled bowl, combine the heavy whipping cream, powdered sugar, and vanilla extract. Use an electric hand mixer on medium-high speed to whip the cream until stiff peaks form.

Gently spoon about one-third of the whipped cream into the lime mixture, folding it in with a rubber spatula to lighten the base. Add the remaining whipped cream and fold very gently just until combined and uniform in color. Avoid over-mixing to keep the mousse light and airy.
Spoon crushed graham cracker crumbs into the bottom of dessert glasses. Pipe or spoon the lime mousse evenly over the crumbs.
Cover the glasses loosely with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow the mousse to set completely.
Garnish each glass with a light dusting of extra graham cracker crumbs, a pinch of lime zest, and a thin lime wheel just before serving.

title: THAI COCONUT & VEGETABLE

ingredients.name: stalks lemongrass (bruised and cut into 2-inch pieces), 1-inch piece galangal or fresh ginger (sliced into coins)
cloves garlic (minced)Flavor Builders: 1 tbsp Thai red curry paste

steps: Sauté the curry paste: Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the red curry paste and minced garlic, stirring constantly for 1 minute until highly fragrant.

Pour in the vegetable broth and coconut milk. Add the lemongrass pieces, galangal slices, and torn kaffir lime leaves. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 5 to 7 minutes to fully infuse the broth.

Toss the shiitake mushrooms, red bell pepper, and broccoli florets into the pot. Simmer for 3 minutes. Add the baby bok choy and cook for an additional 2 minutes until all vegetables are tender-crisp.
Stir in the soy sauce and coconut sugar. Turn off the heat and stir in the fresh lime juice to bring a bright contrast to the rich coconut base.
Remove the woody lemongrass pieces, galangal coins, and kaffir lime leaves from the pot. Ladle the hot soup into deep bowls and garnish generously with fresh cilantro and optional sliced chilis.

title: LEMONGRASS CHICKEN

ingredients.name: boneless, skinless chicken thighs (cut into bite-sized pieces)Lemongrass Marinade: 2 stalks fresh lemongrass (finely minced, white parts only)
chili flakes (optional)Cooking: 1 tbsp vegetable oil (for the pan)
fresh cilantro, lemon or lime wedges

steps: Cut off the tough top leaves and the woody base of the lemongrass stalks. Peel away the dry outer layers until you reach the pale, soft inner core. Finely mince or grate this core to release its aromatic oils.

In a large bowl, whisk together the minced lemongrass, garlic, shallot, fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil, and optional chili flakes. Toss the chicken pieces in the marinade until evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (or up to 4 hours).
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the hot pan in a single layer, discarding excess marinade. Let it cook undisturbed for 4 to 5 minutes to develop a deeply browned, caramelized crust.
Flip the chicken pieces and stir-fry for another 5 to 6 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Transfer the chicken to a serving platter. Garnish with sliced green onions, fresh cilantro, and a squeeze of fresh lemon or lime juice.

title: CHICKPEA & VEGETABLE CURRY

ingredients.name: (15 oz each) chickpeas / garbanzo beans (rinsed and drained)
(14 oz) diced tomatoes (with juices)Aromatics & Oil: 1 tbsp coconut oil (or vegetable oil)
grated fresh gingerSpices: 1.5 tbsp yellow curry powder
large sweet potato (peeled and diced into 1/2-inch cubes)

steps: Sauté the aromatics: Heat the coconut oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion for 5 minutes until soft and translucent. Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for 1 additional minute until highly fragrant.
Add the curry powder, cumin, turmeric, and salt directly to the onion mixture. Stir constantly for 30 seconds to toast the spices, which unlocks their full depth of flavor.

Pour in the canned diced tomatoes (with their juices) and the diced sweet potato cubes. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low, cover the pot, and simmer for 10 minutes until the sweet potatoes begin to soften.

Stir the rinsed chickpeas and chopped red bell pepper into the pot. Pour the full-fat coconut milk over the mixture and stir well to combine. Simmer uncovered for 5 to 7 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly and the sweet potatoes are completely fork-tender.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the fresh baby spinach and cover the pot for 2 minutes, allowing the residual heat to gently wilt the leaves.
Stir in the fresh lime juice to cut through the rich coconut base. Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: GREEN MANGO SALAD WITH POPPYSEED DRESSING

ingredients.name: large green mangoes (unripe and firm to the touch, peeled and julienned)
finely chopped fresh mint or cilantroPoppyseed
neutral oil (like avocado or canola oil)

steps: Combine the neutral oil, apple cider vinegar, honey, Dijon mustard, and salt in a small bowl. Whisk vigorously until the mixture emulsifies. Stir in the poppy seeds and set aside.
Peel the green mangoes. Use a mandoline, julienne peeler, or a sharp knife to slice the firm flesh into thin, matchstick-sized strips, stopping when you reach the hard inner pit.
In a large mixing bowl, toss together the julienned green mangoes, thinly sliced red bell pepper, and red onion.
Pour the poppyseed dressing over the salad components. Toss thoroughly to ensure the mango strips absorb the dressing.
Fold in the fresh chopped mint or cilantro just before serving. Let the salad sit for 5 minutes at room temperature to soften the mango slightly.

title: PINEAPPLE CAKE WITH CITRUS TOPPING

ingredients.name: (20 oz) pineapple slices in juice (drained, reserve 1/4 cup juice)The Cake Batter: 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
whole milkThe Citrus Glaze: 1/4 cup powdered sugar

steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Pour the melted butter directly into the bottom of a 9-inch round cake pan. Sprinkle the brown sugar evenly over the butter.
Lay the drained pineapple slices in a single layer on top of the brown sugar layer. Place one slice in the center and arrange the others around it. Set aside.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
In a large bowl, cream the softened butter and granulated sugar together with an electric mixer until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, followed by the vanilla extract.
Alternate adding the dry flour mixture, milk, and reserved pineapple juice to the wet ingredients, mixing on low speed just until a smooth batter forms.
Spoon the batter carefully over the arranged pineapple slices, smoothing the top with a spatula. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.

Let the cake cool in the pan for exactly 10 minutes. Run a knife around the edges, place a large serving plate upside down over the pan, and quickly invert it. Let the pan sit for 1 minute so the caramel glaze drizzles down before lifting the pan.
Whisk the powdered sugar, lime juice, and orange zest together in a small cup. Drizzle this bright citrus glaze over the warm pineapple cake before slicing.

title: FRENCH LENTIL

ingredients.name: French green lentils (Lentilles du Puy), rinsed and picked over
cloves garlic (minced)Flavor Builders: 2 tbsp tomato paste
dry white or red wine (optional)Liquids: 6 cups low-sodium vegetable or chicken brothHerbs & Seasoning: 1 bay leaf
black pepperBrightener: 1 tbsp fresh lemon juice or red wine vinegar

steps: Sauté the mirepoix: Heat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the diced onion, carrots, and celery. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are soft and the onions are translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and tomato paste. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the tomato paste darkens into a deep brick-red color and smells fragrant.
If using wine, pour it in now to deglaze the pan, using a wooden spoon to scrape up any browned bits stuck to the bottom. Simmer for 2 minutes until most of the alcohol evaporates.

Add the rinsed French green lentils, broth, bay leaf, and thyme sprigs to the pot. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover tightly and simmer for 30 to 35 minutes until the lentils are tender but still hold their shape.Thicken (Optional): Remove the bay leaf and thyme sprigs. For a thicker texture, transfer 1 cup of the soup to a blender, puree until smooth, and stir it back into the pot.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the fresh lemon juice or red wine vinegar and chopped parsley. Season with additional salt and pepper to taste. Ladle into bowls and serve hot.

title: CAPRESE STYLE CHICKEN PLATE

ingredients.name: small chicken breasts (boneless, skinless, pounded to an even 1/2-inch thickness)Chicken Seasoning: 1 tbsp olive oil
thick slices fresh mozzarella cheese (from a log)
high-quality balsamic glaze (store-bought reduction or homemade

steps: Pat the chicken breasts dry with paper towels. Rub both sides evenly with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, dried Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Heat a large, oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken breasts in a single layer. Cook undisturbed for 5 to 6 minutes until a deeply golden crust forms on the bottom.
Flip the chicken breasts over. Immediately top each breast with a slice of fresh tomato, followed by a slice of fresh mozzarella cheese.

Cover the skillet tightly with a lid and reduce the heat to medium-low. Let cook for 4 to 5 minutes until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) and the mozzarella is completely melted and gooey.
Transfer the chicken plates to a clean serving platter and let them rest for 3 minutes to lock in the juices.
Just before serving, tuck fresh basil leaves under and around the melted cheese layer on each breast. Drizzle the glossy balsamic glaze generously across the top of the entire plate.

title: COD WITH LEMON&HERB SAUCE (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: wild -caught cod fillets (about 6 oz each, thick cuts preferred)The Sear: 1 tbsp olive oil
salt, and white pepper (to taste)Lemon & Herb
cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp cold water (slurry for thickening

steps: Pat the cod fillets thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season both sides evenly with onion powder, salt, and white pepper.

Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the cod fillets and sear undisturbed for 3 to 4 minutes until a golden crust forms and the fish naturally releases from the pan.
Carefully flip the fillets using a fish spatula. Cook for another 2 to 3 minutes until opaque throughout and flaky. Transfer to a warm platter and cover loosely with foil.
Turn the skillet down to medium heat. Add the 2 tablespoons of extra-virgin olive oil. Sauté the minced shallots and garlic for 2 minutes until soft and fragrant.
Pour in the vegetable broth, lemon juice, and lemon zest. Bring to a simmer for 2 minutes. Whisk in the cornstarch slurry and let the sauce bubble for 1 minute until it thickens into a glossy glaze.
Turn off the heat. Stir the fresh parsley and dill directly into the warm sauce.
Pour the velvety lemon-herb sauce generously over the seared cod fillets and serve hot.

title: MEDITERRANEAN CHICKPEA

ingredients.name: (15 oz each) chickpeas / garbanzo beans (rinsed and drained)The
orange or red bell pepper (diced)The Brine & Cream: 1/2 cup Kalamata olives (pitted and halved)

steps: Pour the canned chickpeas into a colander. Rinse thoroughly under cold water and let them drain completely. Pat them dry with a paper towel so the dressing sticks to them.
In a small bowl or jar, combine the extra-virgin olive oil, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Whisk vigorously until the dressing emulsifies.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the drained chickpeas, diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, diced red onion, and diced bell pepper.
Toss the halved Kalamata olives and chopped fresh parsley into the bowl. Gently fold in the crumbled feta cheese.
Pour the lemon-oregano dressing over the salad. Toss gently with large spoons until every ingredient is evenly coated.
Let the salad sit at room temperature for 10 to 15 minutes before serving. This allows the chickpeas to absorb the zesty dressing and fields a much deeper flavor.

title: APPLE TART

ingredients.name: pre-made puff pastry (thawed) or 1 unbaked 9-inch pie crust
large Honeycrisp, Granny Smith, or Gala apples (peeled, cored, and very thinly sliced)The Sweetener & Spice: 3 tbsp unsalted butter (melted)

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (204°C). If using puff pastry, roll it out slightly on a sheet of parchment paper into a 10-inch square, then place it on a baking sheet. If using standard pie pastry, press it firmly into a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom.
Toss the thin apple slices in a large bowl with the melted butter, granulated sugar, ground cinnamon, and vanilla extract until every slice is evenly coated.

Starting from the outer edge of the pastry, lay the apple slices down in a tightly overlapping circular pattern, working toward the center. Shingle them tightly; they will shrink slightly as they bake.
Place the tart in the oven and bake for 30 to 35 minutes. It is ready when the pastry edges are a deep golden-brown and the apple slices are tender and slightly caramelized on the tips.
While the tart is hot out of the oven, stir the apricot preserves and hot water together in a small cup until smooth. Use a pastry brush to gently dab the glossy glaze over the warm apples.
Let the tart cool in the pan for 15 minutes before removing the outer ring. Slice and serve warm.

title: BRAISED SHORT RIBS WITH RED WINE SAUCE

ingredients.name: large onion (, chopped (brown, yellow or white))

steps: Preheat oven to 160°C/325°F.
Sprinkle beef all over with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a large ovenproof pot over high heat. Add half the ribs and brown aggressively all over (~5 - 7 min in total). Remove and repeat with remaining ribs, then remove.
Turn heat down to medium. Add onion and garlic into the same pot and cook for 2 minutes.
Add carrot and celery, cook for 5 minutes until carrot is softened and sweet.
Add tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add wine, broth, thyme and bay leaves. Stir until tomato paste is dissolved.
Return beef into liquid, arranging them so they are submerged (Note 3).
Cover with lid and transfer to oven for 3 hours, or until the meat can easily be pried apart with forks. (Note 4 Other cook methods)
Remove beef carefully, keeping the meat on the bone. (Note 4) Cover to keep warm.

Strain all liquid in the pot, pressing juices out of the onion, carrot etc. (Optional - can skip if you don't mind chunky sauce) Return sauce into pot, bring to simmer and stir. Adjust as necessary - simmer to reduce/thicken, add water to thin, season with salt and pepper if needed.
Place beef on serving plate, spoon over sauce. Serve!
